title: Hadoop MapReduce Cluster Architecture
description: The Hadoop MapReduce cluster architecture comprises interconnected components for job management, data processing, and security. The 'counters' directory implements the Abstract Factory pattern for diverse counter groups, ensuring modularity and extensibility. 'filecache' organizes file caching operations, adhering to maintainable design principles. 'jobhistory' uses a layered pattern for job history storage and retrieval. 'lib' encapsulates reusable Java libraries, employing patterns like Adapter, Strategy, Observer, and Composite. 'protocol' provides package documentation, aiding developers. 'security' ensures shuffle and spill encryption, using metadata documentation. 'split' prepares for split operations. 'task/reduce' organizes Reduce phase classes, following modular design. 'util' provides utility classes, adhering to the Utility pattern. This architecture ensures scalability, maintainability, reliability, and security, leveraging Hadoop's core classes, Google's Guava library, and standard Java libraries.
raw_response:
TITLE: Hadoop MapReduce Cluster Architecture

DESCRIPTION: The Hadoop MapReduce cluster architecture comprises interconnected components for job management, data processing, and security. The 'counters' directory implements the Abstract Factory pattern for diverse counter groups, ensuring modularity and extensibility. 'filecache' organizes file caching operations, adhering to maintainable design principles. 'jobhistory' uses a layered pattern for job history storage and retrieval. 'lib' encapsulates reusable Java libraries, employing patterns like Adapter, Strategy, Observer, and Composite. 'protocol' provides package documentation, aiding developers. 'security' ensures shuffle and spill encryption, using metadata documentation. 'split' prepares for split operations. 'task/reduce' organizes Reduce phase classes, following modular design. 'util' provides utility classes, adhering to the Utility pattern. This architecture ensures scalability, maintainability, reliability, and security, leveraging Hadoop's core classes, Google's Guava library, and standard Java libraries.
